newsSince: No material new company-specific news since June 9. The Texas Instruments all-cash acquisition at $231.00/share (signed Feb 4, 2026; ~$7.5B EV; shareholder-approved; close expected 1H 2027) remains the only relevant fact, capping upside at the ~5% arb spread vs the $219.25 price.

sources.label: TI to acquire Silicon Labs (Silabs IR)

verdicts.rankRationale: On the AI-node bottleneck lens, SLAB is a deal-locked analog/RF name with no residual node alpha: TI buyout at $231 caps it at a ~5% arb spread, not bottleneck upside. No news since June 9; continue de-ranking toward removal.
verdicts.bull: Defined $231 cash floor; downside protected if deal closes 1H 2027.
verdicts.neutral: Pure merger-arb; price tracks spread, decoupled from AI-node fundamentals.
verdicts.bear: No residual AI-node alpha; antitrust/close-timing risk on a sub-6% spread.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Regulatory delay/break on the arb spread

verdicts.rankRationale: On the CPO lens SLAB has no co-packaged-optics relevance and is now a capped merger-arb stub; weak direct CPO/optical exposure plus the $231 ceiling leaves no residual alpha. Keep de-ranking toward removal.
verdicts.bull: $231 cash floor caps downside while deal pending.
verdicts.neutral: Arb stub with no CPO linkage; flow- not thesis-driven.
verdicts.bear: Zero co-packaged-optics relevance; belongs off the actionable list.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Deal break with no standalone CPO thesis to fall back on
